description: A still frame captured from law-enforcement body-worn camera footage. The fish-eye-distorted wide-angle frame shows an indoor stairwell/lobby with a red brick wall, a metal handrail, and an orange traffic safety cone. In the foreground a heavyset man in a green athletic jersey bearing the white number '62', tan cargo pants, and lanyards around his neck stands holding white papers (apparently legal/court documents); his face is blurred. To the right two other men are present: one in a grey trench coat and tie (face blurred) holding a phone, and a uniformed officer wearing a dark jacket with a yellow-and-blue shoulder patch reading 'POLICE'. Standard Axon body-camera overlay text is burned into the top of the frame. The image is a raw operational photo with no graphic design, typography styling, agency seals, or slogan treatment applied, so it is not a designed meme/poster. The intent is documentary, illustrating the tweet's narrative of an ICE arrest of a Jamaican national accused of attempted murder who had been bonded out by a Philadelphia police officer. Visible burned-in camera text: 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement', '2026-03-16 09:12:17 -0400', 'AXON BODY 4 D01A3441U'.
